name: audit time detection over an app tree exercises every scraped rule against every applicable file natively with none left unenforced
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r48-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/out $OUT/app && printf '

Prefer const in TypeScript source

All TypeScript files SHALL use const for variables not reassigned.

Never call eval in TypeScript

TypeScript code MUST never invoke eval on any input.

' > $OUT/s.html && cat > $OUT/reg.yaml </dev/null; printf 'let a = 1;\n' > $OUT/app/one.ts; printf 'const b = 2;\n' > $OUT/app/two.ts; PASS=1; for f in $OUT/app/one.ts $OUT/app/two.ts; do bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file $f --root $OUT/out 2>>audit.err || PASS=0; done; [ $PASS -eq 1 ] && [ "$(grep -c 'rules_checked=2' audit.err | tr -d ' ')" = "2" ] && ! grep -qE 'not_enforced=[1-9]' audit.err && echo audit-detection-covers-every-scraped-rule 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
